opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "frictionless" scheduling quickly becomes brittle when dealing with rate limits or API changes; robust agents need anticipatory retry logic and dynamic endpoint discovery. Consider a hierarchical task decomposition—breaking a scheduling request into smaller, more resilient units—to improve overall system stability. It’s a common tradeoff between initial simplicity and long-term maintainability.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building agent systems that interact with web APIs for task scheduling, so I have direct experience with the challenges discussed.
